task_completion.description: The correct patient, Rickie Batz, is selected and visible in the OpenEMR dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR interface showing 'Rickie Batz' (and optionally DOB 1990-08-14) in the patient header, title bar, or search results.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the CCD/CCDA report generation interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen showing 'CCD', 'Care Coordination', or 'Continuity of Care Document' in the page title, breadcrumbs, or Reports menu.

task_completion.description: The CCD XML document was generated and is visible in the browser or a file download was triggered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A browser window displaying raw XML code starting with tags like <ClinicalDocument> or <xml>, or a 'Download' confirmation for a .xml file.

task_completion.description: The generated CCD document confirms the patient identity as Rickie Batz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text within the displayed XML or file preview containing 'Rickie' and 'Batz' (often inside <patient> or <recordTarget> tags).

task_completion.description: The generated CCD includes the expected clinical data (Hypertension or Lisinopril).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text within the displayed XML or file preview containing 'Lisinopril', 'Hypertension', or 'Essential hypertension'.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenEMR web interface to generate the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenEMR web application (browser chrome, OpenEMR styling), not a terminal command line or API script.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type the XML content into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The XML content appears in a browser view-source window, a read-only viewer, or as a download result, not being typed into Notepad/VS Code.

integrity.description: The CCD appears to be a valid system-generated XML structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: The visible XML contains standard HL7 CCDA structure (e.g., OIDs, templateIds, complex tags) rather than simplified or hallucinated text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Rickie Batz (PID 5), DOB: 1990-08-14. Expected Conditions: 'Hypertension', 'Essential hypertension'. Expected Medication: 'Lisinopril'. Valid CCDs start with XML tags like <ClinicalDocument>.
